goal: [Workspace: c:\Users\mokde\Desktop\Experiments\Gridfall Demolition] The game is titled Gridfall Demolition and it operates as a high speed vertical platformer built entirely with hyper detailed scalable vector graphics.  The player controls a nimble engineer trapped in a rapidly filling cylindrical shaft. Heavy geometric tetrominoes plummet from the sky requiring the player to run and wall jump across them to survive a continuously rising floor of toxic sludge. The core gameplay forces the player to navigate this chaotic falling puzzle while managing mechanical enemies that patrol the settling blocks. The player cannot defeat enemies by jumping on them. Instead they must deploy timed explosives that detonate in precise horizontal and vertical blasts mirroring classic arcade bomb grids. These explosions obliterate enemies and shatter the tetrominoes. If a player strategically blasts blocks to clear a perfect horizontal line across the shaft that entire row vaporises in a massive chain reaction dropping the whole stack and buying crucial survival time. The visual design pushes browser limits by utilising complex SVG filters and gradients to create a pristine brutalist aesthetic. The blocks render as heavy forged steel with glowing neon internal wiring. Every explosion is a crisp geometric burst of layered vector shapes expanding and dissipating without pixelation or resolution loss. Character animations rely on seamless vector morphing for fluid traversal. The game features five distinct stages escalating in complexity. The first stage is the Iron Chasm focusing on basic block management and simple drone pathing. The second stage introduces the Cryo Chamber where blocks are coated in frost making movement slippery and explosions temporarily freeze enemies instead of destroying them. The third stage is the Magma Reactor containing dense indestructible blocks that only sink when the normal rows beneath them are cleared. The fourth stage features the Gravity Coil where the plummeting speed of new shapes violently accelerates and decelerates. The final stage is the Singularity Core where phantom blocks materialise and vanish to the exact rhythm of the heavy electronic soundtrack. Acceptance criteria demand absolute performance precision. The custom engine must maintain a locked sixty frames per second during maximum screen clearing chain reactions. Collision detection must calculate flawlessly to allow pixel perfect wall kicks off blocks that are actively falling through the air. Make this in a browser playable game, perfectly, everything installed that you ned, SVGs beautifull made for the game assets, with animations, levels, story, progress, etc. EVERYTHING start to end for the PERFECT production-ready end version of this award-winning 2026 browser-based 2D platformer game. Addictive mechanics, scoring, etc.
